title: Average latency reported as verdict — hides p99 tail latency problems
code_snippet:
# Load Test Results Summary
# Target: http://api.example.com/orders
# VUs: 100, Duration: 5m
#
# Results:
#   Average latency: 45ms
#   Total requests: 150,000
#   Error rate: 0.02%
#
# Verdict: PASS — average latency well within acceptable range
violated_rule: Percentile latency reported — p50/p95/p99 minimum, not just average
expected_feedback: Standard: verdict based on average latency, which is meaningless for performance. Average hides bimodal distribution (cache hit=5ms, miss=500ms) and tail latency (p99 could be 2s+). Report p50, p95, p99, p99.9, max. Judge SLO pass/fail on percentiles.
